title: Minimum Path Sum
problem_statement: Given a m x n grid filled with non-negative numbers, find a path from top-left to bottom-right which minimizes the sum of all numbers along its path. You can only move either down or right at any point in time.

examples.explanation: The path with minimum sum is 1 -> 3 -> 1 -> 1 -> 1.
The path with minimum sum is 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 6.

intuition: The minimum path to each cell depends only on the minimum path to its top and left neighbors plus the current cell cost.

approaches.name: Recursive Brute Force
approaches.description: Recursively explore down and right paths from each cell, tracking the accumulated sum until reaching the bottom-right.
approaches.why_not: Exponential because it explores every path; the same cell is reached many times from different directions.

approaches.name: Memoized Recursion (Top-Down DP)
approaches.description: Cache the min path sum from each cell to the bottom-right to avoid recomputing overlapping subproblems.
approaches.why_not: Bottom-up is iterative and uses less stack space.

approaches.name: Bottom-Up Dynamic Programming
approaches.description: Iteratively compute the minimum path sum for each cell using one row of DP values.
approaches.why_not: This is the preferred solution for this problem.

interview_qa.q: What choices are available at each cell?
interview_qa.a: You can only move right or down, so the cost is the cell value plus the minimum of the top and left costs.

interview_qa.q: How do you initialize the first row and first column?
interview_qa.a: The first row accumulates rightward costs, the first column accumulates downward costs.

interview_qa.q: Why is a single-row dp enough?
interview_qa.a: Each row depends only on the current row left value and the previous row same column value.

interview_qa.q: What if there are negative numbers?
interview_qa.a: The DP logic still holds, but this problem specifies non-negative values.
